test_output_summary: 33 new tests in tests/test_subagent_types_role_name.py all pass; combined with existing tests/test_dispatch_types_frontier_escalation.py 66/66 pass. Pre-existing failures (test_build_system::test_jarvis_version_command, test_phase2_dependencies::test_jarvis_package_imports, test_phase3_dependencies::test_jarvis_version_unchanged, test_get_calendar_events::test_docstring_matches_api_tools_md_section_1_3_byte_for_byte) reproduce on a clean stash and are unrelated to this change (subprocess uses /usr/local/bin/python without uv venv; calendar docstring drift). Ruff lint + format clean on all modified files.
implementation_notes: Added the closed RoleName(str, Enum) to src/jarvis/agents/subagents/types.py alongside the pre-existing AsyncTaskInput, with exactly three lower-case members CRITIC='critic', RESEARCHER='researcher', PLANNER='planner' per DDR-011 / DM-subagent-types §1. No __missing__ override — RoleName('') raises the default ValueError so the subagent graph can map it to the structured 'unknown_role' branch per ADR-ARCH-021 / ASSUM-004. Updated src/jarvis/agents/subagents/__init__.py to re-export both RoleName and AsyncTaskInput. FrontierTarget(str, Enum) in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch_types.py was already present from a prior task with the exact two members required by AC-002 (GEMINI_3_1_PRO='GEMINI_3_1_PRO', OPUS_4_7='OPUS_4_7') — left unchanged and added explicit member-set tests in the new test module. The new tests/test_subagent_types_role_name.py covers both enums for: member set + names + values (AC-001 + AC-002), str inheritance and Enum(value) round-trip + member==value comparison (AC-003), ValueError on '' and other non-members plus assertion that no _missing_/__missing__ override is declared (AC-004), and import-time purity via importlib.reload under patched builtins.open + socket.socket (AC-005). Module-level imports already keep both files free of I/O / LLM construction.
concerns: AC-002's literal values (GEMINI_3_1_PRO='GEMINI_3_1_PRO', OPUS_4_7='OPUS_4_7') diverge from DM-subagent-types §5 which documents provider-prefixed init_chat_model aliases ('google_genai:gemini-3.1-pro', 'anthropic:claude-opus-4-7'). Implementation followed the AC text verbatim (per the user-supplied-value rule) and matches the value already shipped in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch_types.py from the prior task. Worth flagging during /factcheck so future work that needs to call init_chat_model() doesn't pass the literal enum value.

Pre-existing test failures (test_jarvis_version_command, test_jarvis_package_imports, test_jarvis_version_unchanged, test_docstring_matches_api_tools_md_section_1_3_byte_for_byte) are environment- or unrelated-content issues that reproduce on a clean stash; not caused by this change.

completion_promises.evidence: RoleName declares no `_missing_` / `__missing__` override, so default Enum behaviour applies. Verified by tests TestUnknownValueRaisesValueError::test_role_name_empty_string_raises_value_error, ::test_role_name_non_member_value_raises_value_error (parametrised over '', 'CRITIC', 'Critic', 'unknown_role', 'deep_reasoner'), ::test_role_name_has_no_custom_missing_override (asserts neither `_missing_` nor `__missing__` is in `RoleName.__dict__`); equivalent assertions for FrontierTarget cover the symmetric case.
